text: The endoscope is retroflexed to reveal a perforation in the gastric fundus, followed by an attempt to close the perforation endoscopically.
The surgeon uses a double channel gastroscope to advance a polyloop through one channel while placing a hemostatic clip through the other channel, likely targeting the gastric fundus perforation.

The surgeon places a polyloop between the prongs of a hemostatic clip and deploys the clip on one side of the gastric defect. A second clip is placed on the opposite side, also with the polyloop between its prongs. The polyloop is then fully tightened and deployed, visually resembling the placement of an interrupted suture.
The surgeon uses a loop cutter to trim the polyloop tail, ensuring a clear working space. This step is repeated until the defect in the gastric fundus is fully closed.
The surgeon identifies a second perforation distal to the first one in the gastric fundus and closes it using the polyloop and hemostatic clip technique, similar to the previous closure process.
The surgeon examines the repaired perforations in the gastric fundus, confirming complete closure. Green arrows indicate the areas of repair, as verified by a follow-up CT scan with oral contrast.
